## Transformer vs. BERT — Comparison Matrix

| Dimension | Original Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) | BERT (Devlin et al., 2018) |
|---|---|---|
| **Architecture role** | Encoder–decoder: encoder maps input tokens to representations z; decoder auto-regressively generates output symbols one at a time [ev_12722a3120bf0362]. | Encoder-only: a multi-layer bidirectional Transformer encoder, reusing the original Transformer encoder implementation [ev_3540835408466c6e]. |
| **Directionality (masking)** | Decoder uses masked self-attention to prevent leftward information flow, preserving the auto-regressive property (unidirectional, left-to-right in the decoder) [ev_5a465492abf3009b]. | Bidirectional: the MLM objective removes the unidirectionality constraint and fuses left and right context at every layer [ev_0f82af3db4559191]. |
| **Training objective** | Supervised sequence transduction / machine translation: cross-entropy over target tokens generated auto-regressively from the encoder output [ev_12722a3120bf0362]. | Self-supervised pre-training with two objectives: (1) Masked Language Modeling — predict masked tokens from full bidirectional context, with a mixed masking strategy [ev_0f82af3db4559191, ev_45a55f2597027de7]; (2) Next Sentence Prediction (NSP). Followed by task-specific fine-tuning. |

### Key contrasts
- **Architecture:** The original Transformer is a full encoder-decoder for seq2seq generation; BERT drops the decoder and keeps only the Transformer encoder stack.
- **Directionality:** The original Transformer preserves left-to-right generation via masked self-attention in the decoder; BERT is designed to be deeply bidirectional by predicting masked tokens from both sides.
- **Objective:** The original Transformer is trained end-to-end for translation (seq2seq LM cross-entropy); BERT is pre-trained with MLM + NSP on plain text and then fine-tuned, decoupling representation learning from a specific generative task.
summary: Comparison of the original Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) and BERT (Devlin et al., 2018) across architecture, directionality, and training objective, citing 5 corpus evidence items.
